seed: longing vs resolve; order_vs_chaos; cross_current; diagonal_fall; ladder against fault_line; halo_border; dense_vs_empty; single_pixel; intimacy_distance | abstract variation request 352; target batch count 500
title: Interplay of Emotions
reasoning: This artwork conveys a tension between longing and resolve through an interplay of color and structure. The chosen palette, consisting of 'shelter_green', 'warm_brown', 'pale_skin', 'cold_white', and 'open_blue', embodies both warmth and cool detachment, reflecting the emotional dichotomy. The layout emphasizes two central pulsations that pulse away from each other, representing the back-and-forth between desire and determination. The use of broken dots and teeth patterns further enhances this feeling of collision, creating a visual tension that feels intimate yet distant. The main contrast of pressure and quiet space occurs towards the center, which draws the eye and evokes a sense of unresolved motion. Overall, the arrangement embodies the complexity of human emotions—where moments of closeness are separated by vast quiet fields.

layout.name: two_pulses
layout.description: Two small centers pulse toward or away from each other across negative space.

composition.name: near_vs_far
composition.idea: Close paired marks are separated from distant echoes by a wide quiet field.

referenceExample.title: Awe at the Edge
referenceExample.pattern: black hollow center surrounded by cold gold and star-white pressure

generationRules: Pick 1 pattern type for emotion A.
Pick 1 pattern type for emotion B.
Pick 1 shape seed as the central object.
Apply 1 to 6 broken pixels to create imperfection.
Leave at least 10 percent negative space unless the mood is panic, rage, urgency, euphoria, or pressure.
Use the shape seed as an abstract organizing metaphor, not as a literal icon.
Make the two selected pattern types visibly collide, overlap, interrupt, or trade space.
Prefer 4 to 8 distinct hex colors, with at least one quiet field color and one accent color.
Keep the 8x8 grid readable at thumbnail size by preserving clear large-scale structure.
Let one emotion dominate 55 to 70 percent of the square unless the selected layout demands balance.
Use broken pixels as intentional disruptions, scars, repairs, leaks, or false echoes.
Avoid perfectly even distribution unless the chosen composition is control, serenity, clarity, or certainty.
Give the main collision a location: center, edge, corner, diagonal, border, or channel.
generationSpec.negativeSpaceRule: Leave at least 10 percent negative space unless the chosen emotional tone needs dense pressure.
